sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: A $249M drone program could feed Mobilicom orders for the next 5 years
feed.summary: Mobilicom (NASDAQ: MOB) reported its 2025 year-end financial results, highlighting a 7% revenue growth to $3.4 million and a significant 120% increase in cash to $19.1 million. This financial improvement was bolstered by $12.6 million from warrant exercises and equity raises, alongside a 41% reduction in operating cash burn. A major operational highlight is a Tier-1 customer securing a $249 million U.S. Department of War Program of Record, which is expected to drive substantial follow-on orders for Mobilicom's drone systems over the next five years.

feed.title: Momentum ETFs Hit New 52-Week Highs
feed.summary: Momentum ETFs have reached new 52-week highs, driven by strong corporate earnings, AI advancements, and renewed confidence in the tech sector, particularly following Nvidia's blockbuster earnings. The article highlights five specific ETFs—Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (SPMO), iShares MSCI USA Momentum Factor ETF (MTUM), Qraft AI-Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Momentum ETF (AMOM), SPDR S&P 1500 Momentum Tilt ETF (MMTM), and JPMorgan U.S. Momentum Factor ETF (JMOM)—that have benefited significantly from this market momentum. These ETFs leverage an investing strategy focused on continuing market trends to achieve higher returns.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical robots helping surgery centers boost business
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical's da Vinci robots are becoming more prevalent in ambulatory surgery centers (ASCs), helping these facilities grow their business. The article highlights this expansion and its potential impact on the healthcare industry.

feed.title: Moira Buckley starts four-year path to lead advisor group Finseca
feed.summary: Moira Buckley, Western Territory vice president at Prudential Advisors, has been elected as the secretary of Finseca, marking the beginning of a four-year leadership progression that will culminate in her presidency of the organization in 2028-29. With 33 years at Prudential, Buckley is set to advance holistic financial advice and advocate for increased representation, particularly for women, within the financial advisory profession.

feed.title: Broadcom Warns Taiwan Semiconductor Capacity Is Maxed Out
feed.summary: Broadcom has issued a warning that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) capacity is maxed out due to surging AI demand, which is expected to constrain supply through 2026. This shortage is also impacting other components like lasers and printed circuit boards. Despite TSMC's planned capacity expansion, the supply chain is facing bottlenecks, leading customers to secure long-term supply deals.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp stock faces mixed institutional flows amid dividend stability and regional banking strategy shifts
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE: USB) is experiencing mixed institutional investor activity, with some increasing stakes and others significantly reducing them, ahead of its March 31, 2026, dividend record date. The bank's strong dividend yield of 4.02% and its regional, Midwest-focused strategy are highlighted as key strengths in a competitive banking landscape. Despite some European stock declines, analysts maintain positive outlooks, citing reasonable valuations, expected EPS growth, and robust financial health, making it an attractive option for income-focused portfolios.

feed.title: Citigroup Inc. stock faces pressure amid rising credit card charge-offs and banking sector headwinds
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. stock is experiencing pressure due to rising credit card charge-offs and broader banking sector headwinds, despite strong 2025 financial results and ongoing restructuring efforts. While delinquencies remain stable, an increase in net charge-offs in February 2026 highlights growing consumer stress, impacting investor sentiment. Institutional investors, however, show confidence, viewing the bank's transformation and diversified portfolio as potential for long-term value, as the company navigates an uncertain economic landscape.

feed.title: Keep Kraft Heinz In Your Pantry, Not Your Portfolio (NASDAQ:KHC)

However, the company has shown negative total returns due to falling revenues and declining margins over several years, leading to a dismal market sentiment. Given consensus earnings estimates and a downward revision trend, the author recommends investors sell the stock.

feed.title: Explosion Forces Shutdown of Valero’s Port Arthur Refinery
feed.summary: Valero Energy Corp has shut down its 380,000-barrel-per-day oil refinery in Port Arthur, Texas, following an explosion and fire at a diesel hydrotreater unit on Monday evening. The blast, which occurred at a 47,000-bpd 243-diesel hydrotreater unit, was heard miles away, prompting a shelter-in-place order from city officials. Valero confirmed the fire and stated that all personnel were accounted for, with emergency response teams coordinating with local authorities as the fire continued to rage hours after the explosion.

feed.title: Travelers sues Lowe's, says retailer's own actions void vendor coverage
feed.summary: Travelers is suing Lowe's, arguing that Lowe's's actions void coverage under a vendor's policy for a windstorm injury incident. The lawsuit stems from an incident where a sod hut, originally installed by a vendor, was moved and left unsecured by Lowe's employees, leading to a personal injury claim. Travelers contends that Lowe's's direct involvement in altering the product and its failure to re-anchor it, as well as specific policy exclusions and limits, disqualify Lowe's from coverage.

feed.title: DOOR Deadline: DOOR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Masonite International Corporation Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm reminds investors who sold Masonite International Corporation (NYSE: DOOR) common stock between June 5, 2023, and February 8, 2024, of the April 7, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline in a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Masonite made material omissions and misrepresentations regarding Owens Corning's offers to purchase its stock at a premium, while Masonite repurchased shares without disclosing this nonpublic information. Investors who sold during this period may be entitled to compensation and can join the class action by contacting the firm.

feed.title: First Solar Inc. stock faces pressure amid analyst cuts and legal probe as solar sector shifts
feed.summary: First Solar Inc. (FSLR) stock has recently faced selling pressure due to analyst downgrades, insider sales, and an investor investigation by Pomerantz LLP. Despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus among analysts, the company's shares dropped after missing Q4 EPS estimates, though revenue exceeded expectations. The stock's performance is closely watched by US investors as it navigates a volatile renewables market with high institutional ownership and leverages thin-film technology in domestic manufacturing amid evolving clean energy policies.
